mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth since 2010
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, sharp increases in UNRATE precede recessions and market downturns.
failure_condition: If, during periods where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with low and stable inflation indicate robust consumer demand without overheating, creating an ideal environment for corporate revenue growth and higher equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL is 232.77, indicating low inflation.
failure_condition: If, when both conditions are met, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained recovery in housing starts indicates improving consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which has broad positive spillover effects on the economy and equity markets, particularly during a post-recession recovery phase.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 800 (thousands of units) and 3-month average is rising
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST is currently 890.0, showing recovery from crisis lows.
failure_condition: If, during post-recession periods where HOUST is above 800k and rising, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signifies increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, reflecting strong underlying economic activity that typically translates to higher corporate earnings and positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been recovering but not yet showing strong acceleration.
failure_condition: If, when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate, maintained by the central bank, signals an accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating economic growth. This reduces borrowing costs and encourages investment, supporting higher equity valuations.
regime_hint: Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) or Quantitative Easing (QE) environment
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth indicates a strong and expanding economy, which provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 has shown positive growth in recent quarters, though lagged.
failure_condition: If, when GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 2.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong job creation (PAYEMS) and a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which is highly supportive of equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 150k AND UNRATE 3-month average is falling
evidence_refs: fact:Both PAYEMS and UNRATE indicate an improving labor market currently.
failure_condition: If, when both PAYEMS growth and falling UNRATE conditions are met, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.
